hypothesis: If real-time commodity price feeds and geopolitical supply-chain risk indices were integrated as dynamic inputs into the analysis module, then the workflow would predict synthesis route switches—specifically transitions from thermodynamically suboptimal but currently cheap precursors (e.g., CaO/SiO2) to higher-yield alternatives (e.g., Ca3SiO5/Si3N4)—at economically critical price-ratio thresholds that can be empirically identified and validated against historical market disruption events, thereby enabling proactive rather than reactive procurement decisions in industrial solid-state synthesis.
